definition: Use a cheap proxy similarity, sketch, embedding, metadata filter, or coarse feature index to generate candidate train neighbors, then compute the scientifically preferred expensive similarity exactly on those candidates.

scale_profile.best_for: Domains where the defensible scientific similarity is expensive but a calibrated proxy can recover candidates.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Depends on proxy recall and exact reranking cost.
scale_profile.avoid_when: No proxy can be calibrated against the intended similarity.

tunable_parameters.name: proxy_candidate_k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of candidates passed to exact reranking.

tunable_parameters.name: proxy_threshold
tunable_parameters.purpose: Optional lower bound for candidate inclusion.

quality_gates: Measure proxy candidate recall against exact all-pairs on a subset.
Report proxy features separately from final exact similarity values.
Run sensitivity analysis over candidate budget.
failure_modes: Proxy bias can erase the very novelty region SPECTRA should audit.
Candidate budgets can create artificial plateaus in the spectral curve.
Exact rerank cost can still be too high for large candidate sets.
example_usage: Use minhash_lsh_jaccard.py as a transparent two-stage example: LSH proposes candidates and exact Jaccard reranks them before SPECTRA audit.

citations.title: Improved seed design for protein similarity search
citations.evidence: Uses a seed prefilter before more expensive sequence similarity evaluation.

citations.title: A new lower bound for dynamic time warping
citations.evidence: Uses cheap bounds before expensive DTW computations, illustrating the general prefilter-rerank pattern.
